title: Semi-arid climate

infobox.value: is a [dry climate](/wiki/Aridity "Aridity") sub-type. It is located on regions that receive [precipitation](/wiki/Precipitation "Precipitation") below [potential evapotranspiration](/wiki/Evapotranspiration#Potential_evapotranspiration "Evapotranspiration"), but not as low as a [desert climate](/wiki/Desert_climate "Desert climate"). There are different kinds of semi-arid climates, depending on variables such as temperature, and they give rise to different [biomes](/wiki/Biome "Biome").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Climate with precipitation below potential evapotranspiration

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/15/BS_climate.png/500px-BS_climate.png)](/wiki/File:BS_climate.png)

Regions with semi-arid climates

[BSh](#Hot_semi-arid_climates)

[BSk](#Cold_semi-arid_climates)

A **semi-arid climate**, **semi-desert climate**, or **steppe climate** is a [dry climate](/wiki/Aridity "Aridity") sub-type. It is located on regions that receive [precipitation](/wiki/Precipitation "Precipitation") below [potential evapotranspiration](/wiki/Evapotranspiration#Potential_evapotranspiration "Evapotranspiration"), but not as low as a [desert climate](/wiki/Desert_climate "Desert climate"). There are different kinds of semi-arid climates, depending on variables such as temperature, and they give rise to different [biomes](/wiki/Biome "Biome").

## Defining attributes of semi-arid climates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Semi-arid_climate&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Defining attributes of semi-arid climates")]

A more precise definition is given by the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification"), which treats [steppe](/wiki/Steppe "Steppe") climates (*BSh* and *BSk*) as intermediates between [desert climates](/wiki/Desert_climate "Desert climate") (BW) and humid climates (A, C, D) in [ecological](/wiki/Ecological "Ecological") characteristics and agricultural potential. Semi-arid climates tend to support short, thorny or scrubby vegetation and are usually dominated by either [grasses](/wiki/Grass "Grass") or [shrubs](/wiki/Shrub "Shrub") as they usually cannot support forests.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Les_Falaises_de_Bandiagara.jpg/250px-Les_Falaises_de_Bandiagara.jpg)](/wiki/File:Les_Falaises_de_Bandiagara.jpg)

[Sahel](/wiki/Sahel "Sahel") region of [Mali](/wiki/Mali "Mali").

To determine if a location has a semi-arid climate, the precipitation threshold must first be determined. The method used to find the precipitation threshold (in millimeters):[[1]](#cite_note-:1-1)

* multiply by 20 the average annual temperature in degrees Celsius and then
  + add 280 if at least 70% of the total precipitation falls in the summer half of the year (April–September in the northern hemisphere, October–March in the southern hemisphere)
  + add 140 if 30–70% of the total precipitation falls in the summer half of the year
  + add nothing if less than 30% of the total precipitation falls in the summer half of the year

If the area's annual precipitation in millimeters is less than the threshold but more than half or 50% the threshold, it is classified as a *BS* (steppe, semi-desert, or semi-arid climate).[[2]](#cite_note-Hydrology_and_Earth_System_Sciences-2)

Furthermore, to delineate hot semi-arid climates from cold semi-arid climates, a mean annual temperature of 18 °C (64.4 °F) is used as an isotherm. A location with a *BS*-type climate is classified as hot semi-arid (*BSh*) if its mean temperature is above this isotherm, and cold semi-arid (*BSk*) if not.[[1]](#cite_note-:1-1)

## Hot semi-arid climates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Semi-arid_climate&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Hot semi-arid climates")]

"BSh" redirects here. For other uses, see [BSH](/wiki/BSH_(disambiguation) "BSH (disambiguation)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/25/Koppen-Geiger_Map_v2_BSh_1991%E2%80%932020.svg/500px-Koppen-Geiger_Map_v2_BSh_1991%E2%80%932020.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Koppen-Geiger_Map_v2_BSh_1991%E2%80%932020.svg)

Regions with hot semi-arid climates

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/52/Mitchell_grass_downs_post_rain_Boulia_Shire_Queensland_P1070007.jpg/250px-Mitchell_grass_downs_post_rain_Boulia_Shire_Queensland_P1070007.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mitchell_grass_downs_post_rain_Boulia_Shire_Queensland_P1070007.jpg)

Semi-arid climate in [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland"), Australia.

Hot semi-arid climates (type "BSh") tend to be located from the high tens to mid-30s latitudes of the [tropics](/wiki/Tropics "Tropics") and [subtropics](/wiki/Subtropics "Subtropics"), typically in proximity to regions with a [tropical savanna climate](/wiki/Tropical_savanna_climate "Tropical savanna climate") or a [humid subtropical climate](/wiki/Humid_subtropical_climate "Humid subtropical climate"). These climates tend to have hot, or sometimes extremely hot, summers and warm to cool winters, with some to minimal precipitation. Hot semi-arid climates are most commonly found around the fringes of subtropical deserts.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Hot semi-arid climates are most commonly found in [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), the [Indian subcontinent](/wiki/Indian_subcontinent "Indian subcontinent") and the outskirts of the [Iranian plateau](/wiki/Iranian_plateau "Iranian plateau"). In Australia, a large portion of the [Outback](/wiki/Outback "Outback") surrounding the central desert regions lies within the hot semi-arid climate region.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] In South Asia, [India](/wiki/India "India") and parts of [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") experience the seasonal effects of [monsoons](/wiki/Monsoon "Monsoon") and feature short but well-defined [wet seasons](/wiki/Wet_season "Wet season"), but are not sufficiently wet overall to qualify as either a [tropical savanna](/wiki/Tropical_savanna_climate "Tropical savanna climate") or a [humid subtropical](/wiki/Humid_subtropical_climate "Humid subtropical climate") climate.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Hot semi-arid climates can be also found in parts of [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America"), such as most of northern [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"), the [ABC Islands](/wiki/ABC_islands_(Leeward_Antilles) "ABC islands (Leeward Antilles)"), the [rain shadows](/wiki/Rain_shadow "Rain shadow") of [Hispaniola](/wiki/Hispaniola "Hispaniola")'s mountain ranges in the [Dominican Republic](/wiki/Dominican_Republic "Dominican Republic") and [Haiti](/wiki/Haiti "Haiti"), parts of the [Southwestern United States](/wiki/Southwestern_United_States "Southwestern United States") including California's [Central Valley](/wiki/Central_Valley_(California) "Central Valley (California)"), and sections of [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America") such as the *[sertão](/wiki/Sert%C3%A3o "Sertão")*, the [Gran Chaco](/wiki/Gran_Chaco "Gran Chaco"), and the poleward side of the arid deserts, where they typically feature a [Mediterranean](/wiki/Mediterranean_climate "Mediterranean climate") precipitation pattern, with generally rainless summers and wetter winters. They are also found in few areas of [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") surrounding the [Mediterranean Basin](/wiki/Mediterranean_Basin "Mediterranean Basin"). In Europe, BSh climates are predominantly found in southeastern [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) It can also be found primarily in parts of south [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece") but also in marginal areas of [Thessaloniki](/wiki/Thessaloniki "Thessaloniki") and [Chalkidiki](/wiki/Chalkidiki "Chalkidiki") in north [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"),[[6]](#cite_note-Climate_Atlas_of_Greece_(Hellenic_National_Meteorological_Service)-6)[[7]](#cite_note-Monthly_Bulletins-7) most of [Formentera](/wiki/Formentera "Formentera"), marginal areas of [Ibiza](/wiki/Ibiza "Ibiza")[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) and marginal areas of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") in [Sicily](/wiki/Sicily "Sicily"), [Sardinia](/wiki/Sardinia "Sardinia") and [Lampedusa](/wiki/Lampedusa "Lampedusa").

## Cold semi-arid climates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Semi-arid_climate&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cold semi-arid climates")]

"BSk" redirects here. For other uses, see [BSK](/wiki/BSK_(disambiguation) "BSK (disambiguation)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9c/Koppen-Geiger_Map_v2_BSk_1991%E2%80%932020.svg/500px-Koppen-Geiger_Map_v2_BSk_1991%E2%80%932020.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Koppen-Geiger_Map_v2_BSk_1991%E2%80%932020.svg)

Regions with cold semi-arid climates

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Hell_Creek_State_Park.jpg/250px-Hell_Creek_State_Park.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hell_Creek_State_Park.jpg)

Semi-arid climate in [Montana](/wiki/Montana "Montana"), United States.

Cold semi-arid climates (type "BSk") tend to be located in elevated portions of [temperate zones](/wiki/Temperate_zones "Temperate zones") generally from the mid-30s to low 50s latitudes, typically bordering a [humid continental climate](/wiki/Humid_continental_climate "Humid continental climate") or a [Mediterranean climate](/wiki/Mediterranean_climate "Mediterranean climate"). They are also typically found in continental interiors some distance from large bodies of water. Cold semi-arid climates usually feature warm to hot dry summers, though their summers are typically not quite as hot as those of hot semi-arid climates. Unlike hot semi-arid climates, areas with cold semi-arid climates tend to have cold and possibly freezing winters. These areas usually see some [snowfall](/wiki/Snowfall "Snowfall") during the winter, though snowfall is much lower than at locations at similar latitudes with more humid climates.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Areas featuring cold semi-arid climates tend to have higher elevations than areas with hot semi-arid climates, and tend to feature major temperature swings between day and night, sometimes by as much as 20 °C (36 °F) or more. These large [diurnal temperature variations](/wiki/Diurnal_temperature_variation "Diurnal temperature variation") are seldom seen in hot semi-arid climates. Cold semi-arid climates at higher latitudes tend to have dry winters and wetter summers, while cold semi-arid climates at lower latitudes tend to have precipitation patterns more akin to [Mediterranean climates](/wiki/Mediterranean_climate "Mediterranean climate"), with dry summers, relatively wet winters, and even wetter springs and autumns.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Cold semi-arid climates are most commonly found in central Asia and the western US, as well as the Middle East and other parts of Asia. However, they can also be found in Northern Africa, South Africa, sections of South America, sections of interior southern Australia (e.g. [Mildura](/wiki/Mildura "Mildura")) and several sections of Europe: a significant part of [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), much of southern [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), parts of inland [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"), and small parts of [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), and [North Macedonia](/wiki/North_Macedonia "North Macedonia").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

* [Continental climate](/wiki/Continental_climate "Continental climate")
* [Dry climate](/wiki/Dry_climate "Dry climate")
* [Desert climate](/wiki/Desert_climate "Desert climate")
* [Dust Bowl](/wiki/Dust_Bowl "Dust Bowl") (an era of devastating dust storms, mostly in the 1930s, in semi-arid areas on the Great Plains of the United States and Prairies of Canada)
* [Goyder's Line](/wiki/Goyder%27s_Line "Goyder's Line") (a boundary marking the limit of semi-arid climates in the Australian state of South Australia)
* [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification")
* [Palliser's Triangle](/wiki/Palliser%27s_Triangle "Palliser's Triangle") (semi-arid area of Canada)
* [Ustic (Soil Moisture Regime)](/wiki/Ustic "Ustic")
* [Wave height](/wiki/Wave_height "Wave height")
